---
title: "bayes_model"
output: pdf_document
date: "2026-03-23"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r}
colorectal_data <- read.csv('colorectal_cancer_dataset.csv')
head(colorectal_data)
```

```{r}
subset_overall <-colorectal_data[, c(
  
  "Tumor_Size_mm",
  "Healthcare_Costs",
  "Urban_or_Rural",
  "Healthcare_Access",
  "Economic_Classification"
)]
subset_overall$Urban_or_Rural <- as.numeric(factor(subset_overall$Urban_or_Rural))
subset_overall$Healthcare_Access <- as.numeric(factor(subset_overall$Healthcare_Access))
subset_overall$Economic_Classification <- as.numeric(factor(subset_overall$Economic_Classification))
subset_overall$Tumor_Size_mm <- as.numeric(factor(subset_overall$Tumor_Size_mm))
head(subset_b)
head(subset_a)
head(subset_overall)
```

```{r}
# if we're trying to do b | a
# keep only healthcare_costs,  urban or rural, healthcare_access, economic classification for a
# keep only survival prediction for b
# Calculate the covariance matrix
cov_matrix <- cov(subset_overall)

# Print the resulting matrix
print(cov_matrix)
```

```{r}
# p(5 years | healthcare costs , urban or rural, economic classification)
Sigma_aa <- cov_matrix[1, 1]
Sigma_ab <- matrix(cov_matrix[1, -1], nrow=1)
Sigma_ba <- matrix(cov_matrix[-1, 1], nrow=4)
Sigma_bb <- cov_matrix[-1, -1]

mu <- colMeans(subset_overall)
mu_a <- mu[1]
mu_b <- matrix(mu[-1], ncol = 1)
```

```{r}
# in our case, we would be using a random row as y_b and guessing y_a based on it
# for now, just use the first row, change this when actually using
y_b <- matrix(as.numeric(subset_overall[1, -1]), ncol = 1)

# Compute conditional mean and covariance for p(y1| y2 , y3, y4)
Sigma_bb_inv <- solve(Sigma_bb)
mu_agb <- (mu_a + Sigma_ab %*% Sigma_bb_inv %*% (y_b - mu_b))
Sigma_agb <- (Sigma_aa - Sigma_ab %*% Sigma_bb_inv %*% t(Sigma_ab))

print(mu_agb)
print(Sigma_agb)

# Actual tumor size
tumor_size <- 45

# Generate x1 values for plotting
x_line <- seq(c(mu_agb - 3 * sqrt(Sigma_agb)),
              c(mu_agb + 3 * sqrt(Sigma_agb)),
              length.out = 100)

# Compute probability density function
p_agb <- dnorm(x_line, mean = mu_agb, sd = sqrt(Sigma_agb))

# Plot the estimated tumor size distribution
ggplot() +
  geom_line(aes(x = x_line, y = p_agb), color = "blue") +
  geom_vline(aes(xintercept = tumor_size), color = "red", linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_vline(aes(xintercept = mu_agb), color = "green", linetype = "dotted") +
  labs(x = "Health", y = "Probability Density",
       title = "Estimated Tumor Size Distribution")
```

```{r}
# Determine turmor size based on patient data
subset_overall <-colorectal_data[, c(
  "Healthcare_Costs",
  "Economic_Classification",
  "Healthcare_Access",
  "Insurance_Status"
)]

library(MCMCpack)
library(mvnfast)
y <- subset_overall$Healthcare_Costs

X <- model.matrix(Healthcare_Costs ~ factor(Economic_Classification) +
                                      factor(Healthcare_Access) +
                                      factor(Insurance_Status),
                  data = subset_overall)
p <- ncol(X)
prior_w <- matrix(0, nrow = p, ncol = 1)
prior_Sigma <- diag(p) * 1e6
y <- as.matrix(y)
X <- as.matrix(X)
n <- length(y)
library(MCMCpack)

# Now need priors for sigma_y
prior_alpha <- 3
prior_beta <- var(y) * (prior_alpha - 1)
# will need the sample size
n <- length(y)

# Set up markov chain matrices
no_samples <- 1000
gibbs_samples_w <- matrix(0, nrow = no_samples, ncol = p)
gibbs_samples_sigmay <- matrix(0, nrow = no_samples, ncol = 1)

#initialize
ols_fit <- lm(Healthcare_Costs ~ factor(Economic_Classification) +
                                   factor(Healthcare_Access) +
                                   factor(Insurance_Status),
              data = subset_overall)
w_tilde <- matrix(coef(ols_fit), ncol = 1)
sigma2_tilde <- summary(ols_fit)$sigma^2

# run the sampler
for(i in 1:no_samples){
  # conditional posterior parameters for w
  V = solve((1/sigma2_tilde)*t(X)%*%X + solve(prior_Sigma))

  M = V%*%((1/sigma2_tilde)*t(X)%*%y + solve(prior_Sigma)%*%prior_w)
  # sample weights
  w_tilde <- t(rmvn(1, M, V)) # rmvn gives w^T
  
  # conditional posterior parameters for sigma2
  A = (2*prior_alpha + n)/2
  B = (2*prior_beta + t(y - X%*%w_tilde)%*%(y - X%*%w_tilde))/2
  # sample sigma2
  sigma2_tilde <- max(rinvgamma(1, A, B), 1e-6)
  
  # store them
  gibbs_samples_w[i,] <- w_tilde
  gibbs_samples_sigmay[i,] <- sigma2_tilde
}

# check convergence and mixing of weights
plot(gibbs_samples_w[,2], type = "l") # change the index to examine the 7 weights
acf(gibbs_samples_w[,2]) # change the index to examine the 7 weights

# check convergence and mixing of variance term
plot(gibbs_samples_sigmay, type = "l")
acf(gibbs_samples_sigmay)

# converged quickly, very good mixing. Not even going to bother with burn-in or thinning
# though, you may need to in practice

# visualize posteriors?
hist(gibbs_samples_sigmay, main = "Posterior of Variance of Health Care Costs")
for (j in 1:p) {
  hist(gibbs_samples_w[,j], main = paste("Posterior of weight", j))
}

# Generating predictive distribution for Healthcare
pred <- numeric(nrow(gibbs_samples_w))
for(i in 1:nrow(gibbs_samples_w)){
  pred[i] <- rnorm(1, mean = t(gibbs_samples_w[i,]) %*% X[1,], sd = sqrt(gibbs_samples_sigmay[i]))
}
hist(pred, main = "Posterior Predictive Distribution")



cat("y mean:", mean(y), "\n")          # should be ~66,000
cat("y range:", range(y), "\n")        # should be 25,033 to 119,469
cat("X dimensions:", dim(X), "\n")     # should be 100 x p
cat("p:", p, "\n")
cat("prior_beta:", prior_beta, "\n")
```
